pr.title: Bring ftCo_800BBED4 to a single regswap
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Small matching-focused cleanup in Yoshi Egg common fighter code. The PR refactored ftCo_800BBED4 in src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_YoshiEgg.c by removing artificial nested scopes, dummy byte arrays, a redundant local JObj variable, and a manual NULL assert wrapper around HSD_JObjGetScale. It hoisted the Vec3 scale local to function scope and inserted PAD_STACK(16) in the later block, reducing the function to the PR title's stated goal of a single remaining register swap.

key_files.reason: Only changed file; contains the ftCo_800BBED4 refactor for Yoshi Egg common fighter behavior.

what_changed: Replaced an artificial `u8 _[8] = { 0 };` padding local at the top of ftCo_800BBED4 with a real `Vec3 scale` local.
Flattened a nested block that previously declared `Vec3 scale`, `u8 _[4]`, `float size`, and a shadowing `HSD_JObj* jobj`.
Assigned `scale.x = scale.y = scale.z = fp->co_attrs.xBC.size` directly instead of routing through a temporary `float size`.
Called `HSD_JObjSetScale(fp->x20A0_accessory, &scale)` directly instead of assigning `fp->x20A0_accessory` to a block-local `jobj`.
Added `PAD_STACK(16)` inside the later block containing `struct UNK_SAMUS_S2 unk_struct`, indicating explicit stack-shape control replaced previous dummy locals.
Removed a local manual NULL check and `__assert("jobj.h", 823, "jobj")` wrapper before `HSD_JObjGetScale`; the code now calls `HSD_JObjGetScale(jobj, &fp->mv.co.yoshiegg.x18)` directly.
No behavior-level intent was stated beyond matching/regswap improvement; there were no PR comments or review comments in the provided slice.
smart_moves: Used real semantic locals (`Vec3 scale`) where possible instead of anonymous dummy arrays, making the function easier to read while still serving matching goals.
Moved from ad hoc padding variables to an explicit `PAD_STACK(16)`, which documents that the stack adjustment is intentional and matching-related.
Flattened artificial nested scopes after they were no longer needed for register allocation, reducing source noise without changing the visible call sequence.
Removed a redundant temporary `float size`; chained component assignment preserved the intended all-axis uniform scale and likely improved register allocation.
Avoided shadowing the function-scope `jobj` with another `HSD_JObj* jobj`, reducing confusion in a function already sensitive to register placement.
decomp_lessons: When closing in on a match, replace anonymous stack fillers with `PAD_STACK(n)` once the needed stack delta is known; it is more searchable and communicates intent.
Nested scopes, dummy byte arrays, and redundant temporaries can be useful temporary matching tools, but should be removed when a cleaner arrangement reaches the same or better match.
Hoisting a struct local like `Vec3 scale` to the right scope can affect stack/register allocation and eliminate the need for extra fake locals.
Directly using struct fields such as `fp->x20A0_accessory` can avoid extra locals that perturb register allocation.

Do not preserve manual assert wrappers solely as source clutter if the target library call or surrounding code no longer needs that structure for matching; however, this PR provides no discussion explaining the assert removal beyond the diff.
naming_conventions: Function remains named `ftCo_800BBED4`, consistent with unresolved ftCommon symbol naming using address suffixes.
Yoshi Egg state fields use the path `fp->mv.co.yoshiegg.*`, including `scale`, `x14`, `xC`, and `x18`.
Common fighter callbacks and helpers retain `ftCommon_` and `ftCo_` prefixes, e.g. `ftCommon_SetAccessory`, `ftCo_800BC438`, and `ftCo_800BBCC0`.
Yoshi-specific helper names in this slice use `ftYs_` prefixes, e.g. `ftYs_SpecialN_8012CDD4`, `ftYs_Shield_8012CCE0`, and `ftYs_Shield_8012CD00`.
The explicit padding macro is spelled `PAD_STACK(16)`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Primary tactic was register-allocation cleanup: the PR title explicitly says the function was brought to a single regswap.
Removed dummy stack arrays (`u8 _[8]`, `u8 _[4]`) and substituted a targeted `PAD_STACK(16)` where padding was still needed.
Flattened nested scopes to alter lifetime/register pressure around `scale`, accessory setup, and scale assignment.
Eliminated temporary locals (`float size`, block-local accessory `jobj`) that likely caused unnecessary register movement.
Kept the broader operation order intact: state setup, accessory creation and scaling, collision/intangible setup, Yoshi shield values, scale capture, and callback assignment.
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs replacing dummy `u8 _[]` locals with `PAD_STACK` for stack matching.
Search for remaining `ftCo_800BBED4` regswap notes or later renames after PR 1337.
Look for other ftCommon Yoshi Egg functions using `fp->mv.co.yoshiegg` fields to infer better field names.
Search for cases where manual `__assert("jobj.h", 823, "jobj")` wrappers were removed or required for matching.
